resource.note: scarce — reserve for higher-acuity now-tier patients
single MD; multiple now-tier patients ahead

warnings: Several now-tier cases ahead in queue; this patient may experience further delay
Low-grade fever 37.6°C with RLQ peritoneal signs — re-screen for sepsis if vitals shift

hypotheses.name: Other surgical abdomen (diverticulitis, ureteric colic, bowel pathology)
Early intra-abdominal sepsis
rules_against: does not meet R-SEPSIS-01 criteria (only 1 of 2 SIRS — temp <38, HR <90 borderline, RR 18, SBP 134, alert)

monitor.action: Establish IV access, draw CBC/CMP/lipase/lactate, urinalysis; keep NPO
monitor.reason: Likely surgical abdomen — labs and NPO status prepare for imaging and possible OR.

monitor.action: Reassess vitals and abdominal exam q30min; recheck temp, watch for rebound/rising HR
monitor.reason: Detect progression to peritonitis or sepsis early while awaiting MD/imaging.

escalate_or_redirect.action: Notify on-call surgery for evaluation and order CT abdomen/pelvis with contrast
escalate_or_redirect.reason: RLQ pain with guarding warrants surgical consult; surgery ETA 15min aligns with imaging turnaround.

explanation.summary: 52M with 6h RLQ pain, guarding, nausea, low-grade fever, pain 7/10, vitals otherwise stable. Picture is concerning for acute appendicitis or other surgical abdomen requiring labs + CT + surgical consult (≥2 resources). No vital red flags and does not meet sepsis or ESI-2 high-risk criteria, so tier 'soon' (ESI 3) is appropriate, with conservative monitoring for escalation.

citations.signal: stable vitals + ≥2 resources (labs, CT, surgical consult)
screen applied — does not meet ≥2 SIRS criteria; monitor

checklist.task: Place IV, send CBC/CMP/lipase/lactate/UA, type & screen
Keep NPO and document last meal time
MD evaluation and order CT abdomen/pelvis with IV contrast
Page surgery on-call for RLQ peritoneal signs
Reassess vitals, pain, abdominal exam q30min
Re-triage if SBP<100, HR>110, T≥38.5, or rebound develops

why_this_tier: Likely needs ≥2 resources (labs + CT + surgical consult) — fits ESI 3
Does not meet R-SEPSIS-01 or R-ESI-T2 high-risk thresholds currently
Pain 7/10 but without concerning vitals — does not bump to ESI 2
would_upgrade_if: Rebound tenderness, rigidity, or worsening peritoneal signs
would_downgrade_if: Pain resolves, exam benign on reassessment, labs unremarkable
Clear non-surgical etiology identified (e.g., simple GERD/musculoskeletal)
